body.title: Fix test_memory_scope.py::test_credential_ref_not_in_portable JSONDecodeError (blocks pe_test pre-flight)
body.result: Extracted _decode_payload helper at cortex.py — tries json.loads, falls through to raw value on parse failure. 15/15 memory_scope tests pass. Unblocks pe_test pre-flight for worker=igor tickets. Commit 1e25cf1b.
body.description:
Pre-existing test failure in tests/test_memory_scope.py::test_credential_ref_not_in_portable with a JSONDecodeError at `self.scan_once(s, idx)`. This test has been broken for at least weeks — multiple agents this session have called it out as 'pre-existing, unrelated'.

Why it matters now: pe_chain's pre-flight test-suite check (T-pe-chain-preflight-test) correctly ESCALATES when the suite is broken — preventing an Igor-worker from modifying code on top of broken tests. That's good safety behavior, but it means EVERY worker=igor ticket will escalate until this one test is fixed or quarantined. Without this fix, the worker_mode=igor path is effectively dead-on-guardrail.

Fix shape: (a) investigate the JSONDecodeError — probably a test fixture that loads JSON and gets bad data, or a stale serialized snapshot; (b) fix the root cause if trivial; (c) if nontrivial: quarantine the test with pytest.skip + a new tracking ticket, but document clearly so it doesn't rot.

Scope: one test file. Do NOT change the broader memory-scope semantics — just restore the test.

test_plan: The test is the test. Running it after fix must pass. Also run full suite to confirm no collateral regressions (other memory_scope tests).

Context: discovered during T-worker-dispatch-validation re-run (2026-04-21 11:28). See lab/design_docs/validation/T-worker-dispatch-validation-2026-04-21.md for dispatch-capture evidence.
